title: Peristethium phaneroneurum
intro: Peristethium phaneroneurum, also known as 'Mexican Rose', is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Bioactive constituents of Peristethium phaneroneurum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Peristethium phaneroneurum is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a syrup or tincture to treat various ailments.
taxonomy: Peristethium phaneroneurum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. This family is commonly known as the sky flower or globe mallow family. The genus Peristethium is a part of the Polemoniaceae family, which consists of shrubs and herbs. Peristethium phaneroneurum is native to Mexico and parts of Central America. The species is known to be a small, shrubby plant with a limited distribution range.
